Diagnostician proposed Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. QC verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must reevaluate the grade assignment considering the highest mitotic count reported by the Cross-Slide-Aggregator and address the ambiguous features for a more robust diagnosis.

Comment

Given the grade heterogeneity and close margins, further evaluation with Ki-67 and CD31 IHC is recommended. Molecular profiling for KRAS/NRAS/BRAF mutations and MSI status is advised for therapeutic implications. Compared to 12 similar TCGA cases: 5-year OS ~45%.

AI-assisted analysis (PathMind v0.2 · AMD MI300X · 2026-05-08). Must be reviewed by a licensed pathologist before clinical use.

debate_rounds.argument: Given the inconsistencies in grading and ambiguous features, the primary diagnosis has been revised to pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma, which is more aligned with the evidence from Histo-B. The differential includes acinar cell carcinoma and leiomyosarcoma, considering the conflicting findings from both histopathology syntheses. The recommended IHC panel aims to confirm the diagnosis and distinguish between the differentials.

[MINOR] Ambiguous features not fully addressed

debate_history.diagnosis: Leiomyosarcoma
debate_history.thinking: Chain-of-thought reasoning for primary diagnosis: The fascicular growth pattern and myxoid stroma are highly suggestive of leiomyosarcoma, supported by the mitotic activity. However, the subtle acinar variant morphology noted in Histo-B introduces ambiguity. The lack of strong literature support for fascicular growth in TCGA cases further complicates the diagnosis. literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a fascicular growth pattern is not strongly supported by the TCGA cases provided, which include ductal carcinoma in situ, lobular carcinoma, and pancreatic carcinomas without prominent fascicular features. The literature primarily focuses on Ki-67 as a proliferation marker across various tumor types, but does not specifically address fascicular growth patterns. Further investigation into the specific morphological characteristics is recommended.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: Illustrates high-grade DCIS with cribriform and solid growth patterns, not fascicular.

used_papers.title: Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II
used_papers.snippet:
Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II

Female 64y. Invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type. 25mm. Single-file growth pattern, E-cadherin loss. Grade II. ER+, PR+, HER2-, Ki-67 12%. Multifocal pattern (3 foci). Margin involved laterally (R1). 0/10 nodes. pT2 pN0. Recommend repeat excision and adjuvant endocrine therapy
used_papers.relevance: Describes invasive lobular carcinoma with single-file growth, not fascicular.

suggested_papers.title: Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
suggested_papers.relevance: Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with perineural invasion, providing an alternative differential diagnosis.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a solid growth pattern with subtle acinar variant architecture is supported by the presence of multifocal grade II pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma in TCGA-PA-A5YG and the solid growth pattern seen in the pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 case TCGA-PA-NEC-01. The high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (TCGA-BR-DCIS-01) also exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns, which may share some morphological features. The invasive ductal carcinoma of breast (TCGA-BR-A4QG) provides additional context on grading and biomarker expression in solid growth patterns.

Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of glandular with cribriform areas is supported by the presence of a high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) case with cribriform and solid growth patterns in the TCGA dataset. However, the differential diagnosis includes pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma, which shares some histological features but requires distinct immunohistochemical markers for accurate identification. The provided literature does not directly address glandular with cribriform areas but highlights the importance of distinguishing between various neoplasms.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case provides a high-grade DCIS example with cribriform and solid growth patterns, supporting the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
used_papers.relevance: This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma, emphasizing the need for immunohistochemical markers.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case provides a high-grade DCIS example with cribriform and solid growth patterns, supporting the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
used_papers.relevance: This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma, emphasizing the need for immunohistochemical markers.

debate_history.thinking: Chain-of-thought reasoning for primary diagnosis: The presence of solid with glandular formations, grade III heterogeneity, and suspected lymphovascular invasion strongly support a diagnosis of PDAC. However, the subtle acinar variant morphology raises consideration for acinar cell carcinoma, although the overall features are more consistent with PDAC. The literature suggests NET and SPN as part of the differential, but the provided slides lack definitive features supporting these diagnoses.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of 'Solid with glandular formations' suggests a differential diagnosis including pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (NETs) and solid pseudopapillary neoplasms (SPNs). The TCGA case TCGA-PA-NEC-01 supports this hypothesis with a trabecular and solid growth pattern. Immunohistochemistry is crucial for distinguishing between these entities, as highlighted in PMID 27784972.

Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF

debate_history.argument: Primary: Invasive ductal carcinoma, borderline high-grade — II (tubule formation: 2, nuclear pleomorphism: 2, mitoses: 2) — 8500/3

challenges: Grade assignment inconsistency between Synthesis A and Synthesis B
Dominant pattern discrepancies between syntheses
Lymphovascular invasion and necrosis reporting inconsistencies
debate_history.argument: Verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must resolve grade assignment and dominant pattern discrepancies, and ensure consistent reporting of key global features.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of spindle cell proliferation with myxoid change and glandular with cribriform areas does not align closely with the provided TCGA cases or PubMed articles. The closest differential diagnoses from the TCGA cases include IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis and pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2, but neither perfectly matches the described morphology. The PubMed articles do not provide direct evidence for the working hypothesis.

IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis (DDx case)

used_papers.relevance: Illustrates IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis as a differential diagnosis.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
used_papers.relevance: Illustrates pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 as another differential diagnosis.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a solid growth pattern is supported by the presence of cribriform and solid growth patterns in the high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) case TCGA-BR-DCIS-01. The Ki-67 proliferation index is highlighted as a significant biomarker for grading and predicting outcomes across various tumor types, as seen in multiple studies. The solid growth pattern in DCIS suggests a more aggressive phenotype requiring careful monitoring and appropriate therapeutic interventions.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade DCIS, aligning with the working hypothesis.

suggested_papers.title: Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II

Female 64y. Invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type. 25mm. Single-file growth pattern, E-cadherin loss. Grade II. ER+, PR+, HER2-, Ki-67 12%. Multifocal pattern (3 foci). Margin involved laterally (R1). 0/10 nodes. pT2 pN0. Recommend repeat excision and adjuvant endocrine therapy
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a single-file growth pattern in lobular carcinoma, offering a contrast to the solid growth pattern in the working hypothesis.

used_papers.relevance: Illustrates a pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 with trabecular and solid growth patterns.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.

However, the differential diagnosis includes pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma, which shares some histological features but requires distinct immunohistochemical markers for accurate identification. used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This TCGA case provides a high-grade DCIS example with cribriform and solid growth patterns, supporting the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
used_papers.relevance: This TCGA case illustrates the differential diagnosis between pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma and acinar adenocarcinoma, emphasizing the need for immunohistochemical markers.

debate_history.thinking: Revised chain-of-thought reasoning for primary diagnosis considering the highest mitotic count and subtle acinar variant morphology without altering the primary diagnosis.
debate_history.argument: Primary: Invasive ductal carcinoma of no special type (IDC-NST) — III (tubule formation: 3, nuclear pleomorphism: 3, mitoses: 3) — 8500/3

challenges: Grade III assignment may be inconsistent across slides
Discrepancies in margin status and LVI reporting
debate_history.argument: Verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must justify grade III for the full case given discordant slide 7 mitotic count; Cross-Slide-Aggregator should resolve discrepancies in margin status and LVI reporting

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of infiltrating ductal carcinoma NST with a solid growth pattern is supported by the presence of similar cases in the TCGA dataset, particularly TCGA-BR-A4QG, which exhibits SBR grade III features including high mitotic activity and lymphovascular invasion. The literature emphasizes the importance of grading systems like SBR for prognosis and treatment planning, as seen in PMID 2551477 and PMID 17458558. These findings underscore the need for accurate grading and consideration of biomarkers such as Ki-67 for treatment decisions.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case shows a high-grade IDC with solid growth patterns similar to the working hypothesis.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a glandular pattern suggests a differential diagnosis including pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (NETs) and solid-pseudopapillary neoplasms (SPNs). The TCGA case TCGA-PA-NEC-01 supports this hypothesis with its trabecular and solid growth pattern, while the literature highlights the importance of immunohistochemical markers like synaptophysin and chromogranin for distinguishing NETs from other glandular lesions.

used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
used_papers.relevance: This TCGA case illustrates a pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma with a trabecular and solid growth pattern, supporting the glandular hypothesis.

A unified assessment of LVI was provided, considering the suspected LVI in slide 0 and the lack of assessment in slide 1.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a solid growth pattern with subtle acinar variant is supported by the presence of multifocal grade II pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma in TCGA-PA-A5YG, which exhibits a similar solid growth pattern and perineural invasion. The high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (TCGA-BR-DCIS-01) also shows a cribriform and solid growth pattern, though it lacks an invasive component. The invasive ductal carcinoma of breast (TCGA-BR-A4QG) further supports the consideration of solid growth patterns in different contexts.

debate_rounds.argument: Considering the discordant findings between slides and the lack of clear LVI assessment, a grade II SBR was assigned to reflect the overall tumor characteristics. The solid growth pattern with subtle acinar variant supports IDC-NST as the primary diagnosis, while the presence of necrosis and mitotic activity in slide 1 suggests a higher grade in that particular area. The differential diagnoses include pancreatic acinar cell carcinoma due to the solid growth pattern with acinar variant, although the breast origin makes this less likely. High-grade ductal carcinoma in situ is considered due to the cribriform and solid growth pattern, but the invasive component in the current case excludes this diagnosis.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a solid growth pattern with subtle acinar variant is supported by the presence of multifocal grade II pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma in TCGA-PA-A5YG, which exhibits a similar solid growth pattern and perineural invasion. The high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (TCGA-BR-DCIS-01) also shows a cribriform and solid growth pattern, though it lacks an invasive component. The invasive ductal carcinoma of breast (TCGA-BR-A4QG) further supports the consideration of solid growth patterns in different contexts.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a glandular with subtle acinar variant is supported by the presence of a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma case (TCGA-PA-A5YG) with a multifocal pattern and grade II characteristics. Additionally, the invasive ductal carcinoma of breast (TCGA-BR-A4QG) shares some morphological features like high-grade characteristics and nuclear pleomorphism. These cases highlight the importance of immunohistochemistry for accurate differentiation.

used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
used_papers.relevance: This case provides a direct example of a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with multifocal and grade II characteristics.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case shares morphological features such as high-grade characteristics and nuclear pleomorphism, aiding in the differential diagnosis.

debate_rounds.argument: Given the subtle acinar variant architecture and the literature support for pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, the primary diagnosis was revised. The grade was adjusted to 'borderline high-grade' to reconcile the inconsistencies between Histo-A and Histo-B. The differential diagnosis now includes pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma and leiomyosarcoma, with the former being less likely given the absence of typical neuroendocrine features.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of 'Solid and glandular' morphology suggests a differential diagnosis including pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (NETs) and solid-pseudopapillary neoplasms (SPNs). TCGA case TCGA-PA-NEC-01 supports this hypothesis with a trabecular and solid growth pattern in a pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma. Immunohistochemical markers such as synaptophysin and chromogranin are crucial for distinguishing between these entities.

debate_rounds.argument: Considering the grade heterogeneity and the presence of subtle acinar variant morphology, the primary diagnosis has been revised to invasive ductal carcinoma, acinar variant. The grade assignment remains III due to the overall high-grade features and the highest mitotic count observed in any slide. The differential diagnosis has been updated to give more weight to the acinar variant morphology and its implications for the final diagnosis.

However, the subtle acinar variant morphology observed in some areas introduces ambiguity, suggesting the need for further investigation with IHC markers. literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of mixed glandular with cribriform and solid growth patterns is supported by the TCGA case TCGA-BR-DCIS-01, which exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ. The presence of central necrosis and calcifications further aligns with the complex morphology described. However, the TCGA case TCGA-PA-NEC-01 highlights the importance of distinguishing between neuroendocrine tumors and other solid growth patterns through immunohistochemistry.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case demonstrates cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ, matching the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
used_papers.relevance: This case underscores the need for immunohistochemical differentiation between neuroendocrine tumors and other solid growth patterns.

msi_panel.rationale: MSI testing is recommended for all colorectal cancers and may have relevance in this case given the morphological complexity.

treatment_implications.immunotherapy: Not typically indicated for this histotype unless MSI-high or dMMR.

The possibility of neuroendocrine differentiation has been given more weight in the differential diagnosis due to the presence of trabecular patterns and cribriform areas. Further IHC markers such as Synaptophysin, Chromogranin A, and CD56 are recommended to confirm or exclude neuroendocrine differentiation.

The presence of necrosis and borderline high-grade features further support this diagnosis. However, the trabecular pattern with thickened plates raises the possibility of neuroendocrine involvement, which is why pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma is included in the differential diagnosis. literature.key_findings: The trabecular pattern with thickened plates and glandular with cribriform areas suggests a complex morphology potentially involving both neuroendocrine and ductal components. The TCGA case TCGA-PA-NEC-01 demonstrates a trabecular and solid growth pattern in a pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma, providing a relevant differential diagnosis. The presence of cribriform areas in TCGA-BR-DCIS-01 supports consideration of high-grade ductal carcinoma.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
used_papers.relevance: Illustrates trabecular and solid growth patterns in a neuroendocrine carcinoma.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: Demonstrates cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade ductal carcinoma.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a solid growth pattern is supported by the presence of cribriform and solid growth patterns in the high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) case TCGA-BR-DCIS-01. The Ki-67 proliferation index is highlighted as a significant biomarker for grading and predicting outcomes across various tumor types, as seen in multiple studies. The solid growth pattern in DCIS suggests a more aggressive phenotype requiring careful monitoring and appropriate therapeutic interventions.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade DCIS, aligning with the working hypothesis.

suggested_papers.title: Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II

Female 64y. Invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type. 25mm. Single-file growth pattern, E-cadherin loss. Grade II. ER+, PR+, HER2-, Ki-67 12%. Multifocal pattern (3 foci). Margin involved laterally (R1). 0/10 nodes. pT2 pN0. Recommend repeat excision and adjuvant endocrine therapy
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a single-file growth pattern in lobular carcinoma, offering a contrast to the solid growth pattern in the working hypothesis.

The differential diagnosis includes pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma and pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma, but these are less likely given the lack of specific markers and features. The grade has been revised to II based on the lower mitotic count reported by the Aggregator. The staging has been reconsidered based on the additional features such as perineural invasion and the dominant pattern described by Histo-B.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade DCIS, aligning with the working hypothesis.

suggested_papers.title: Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II

Female 64y. Invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type. 25mm. Single-file growth pattern, E-cadherin loss. Grade II. ER+, PR+, HER2-, Ki-67 12%. Multifocal pattern (3 foci). Margin involved laterally (R1). 0/10 nodes. pT2 pN0. Recommend repeat excision and adjuvant endocrine therapy
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a single-file growth pattern in lobular carcinoma, offering a contrast to the solid growth pattern in the working hypothesis.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of spindle cell proliferation with myxoid change, fascicular growth pattern, and papillary-cystic pattern suggests a complex morphology potentially indicative of a variant of infiltrating ductal carcinoma. The TCGA case TCGA-BR-A4QG, featuring SBR grade III IDC with lymphovascular invasion, aligns closely with the aggressive features noted. The literature emphasizes the importance of grading systems like SBR for prognosis, with PMIDs 17458558 and 2551477 highlighting the significance of nuclear grade and proliferation indices.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: Grade III IDC with lymphovascular invasion matches the aggressive features of the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Grading invasive ductal carcinoma of the breast: advantages of using automated proliferation index instead of mitotic count.
used_papers.snippet:

used_papers.relevance: Discusses the advantages of using automated proliferation index for grading invasive ductal carcinoma.

suggested_papers.relevance: Introduces a new score for grading ductal breast carcinomas based on cytological parameters.

suggested_papers.title: A comparative and evaluative study of two cytological grading systems in breast carcinoma with histological grading: an important prognostic factor.

suggested_papers.relevance: Compares two cytological grading methods in breast carcinoma and correlates them with histological grading.

These cases highlight the importance of immunohistochemistry for accurate differentiation.

used_papers.relevance: This case provides a direct example of a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with multifocal and grade II characteristics.

Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF

debate_history.thinking: Given the grade heterogeneity and the disagreement on the dominant pattern between Histo-A and Histo-B, the primary diagnosis was revised to 'Invasive ductal carcinoma with mixed growth patterns'. This reflects the complexity of the case and the need for further investigation. The grading was adjusted to II-III due to the inconsistencies across slides. The ambiguous features were addressed by acknowledging the disagreement on the dominant pattern and providing a rationale for choosing the solid

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a solid growth pattern is supported by the presence of cribriform and solid growth patterns in the high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) case TCGA-BR-DCIS-01. The Ki-67 proliferation index is highlighted as a significant biomarker for grading and predicting outcomes across various tumor types, as seen in multiple studies. The solid growth pattern in DCIS suggests a more aggressive phenotype requiring careful monitoring and appropriate therapeutic interventions.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade DCIS, aligning with the working hypothesis.

suggested_papers.title: Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II

Female 64y. Invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type. 25mm. Single-file growth pattern, E-cadherin loss. Grade II. ER+, PR+, HER2-, Ki-67 12%. Multifocal pattern (3 foci). Margin involved laterally (R1). 0/10 nodes. pT2 pN0. Recommend repeat excision and adjuvant endocrine therapy
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a single-file growth pattern in lobular carcinoma, offering a contrast to the solid growth pattern in the working hypothesis.

suggested_papers.title: A comparative and evaluative study of two cytological grading systems in breast carcinoma with histological grading: an important prognostic factor.
suggested_papers.snippet:
A comparative and evaluative study of two cytological grading systems in breast carcinoma with histological grading: an important prognostic factor.

Cytonuclear gradings in the breast carcinoma raise the level of FNA reportage and improves patient management. Our aim was to evaluate and compare two cytological grading
suggested_papers.relevance: This paper compares cytological grading methods with histological grading, providing additional insights into diagnostic approaches.

suggested_papers.title: Scarff-Bloom-Richardson (SBR) grading: a pleiotropic marker of chemosensitivity in invasive ductal breast carcinomas treated by neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
suggested_papers.snippet:
Scarff-Bloom-Richardson (SBR) grading: a pleiotropic marker of chemosensitivity in invasive ductal breast carcinomas treated by neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

The Scarff-Bloom-Richardson (SBR) grade, an important prognostic factor in breast cancer, was also associated with cell proliferation, a consistent indicator of resp
suggested_papers.relevance: This study explores the association between SBR grade and response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy, offering potential treatment considerations.

debate_rounds.argument: Given the grade heterogeneity and the presence of subtle acinar variant architecture, the primary diagnosis has been revised to IDC-NST with acinar variant features. The SBR grade is adjusted to II-III to reflect the variability across slides. The differential diagnosis now includes acinar cell carcinoma and pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma due to the acinar variant features, although these are less likely given the overall context.
[MAJOR] Grade III assignment may be inconsistent with some slide findings

debate_history.thinking: Chain-of-thought reasoning for primary diagnosis: The dominant pattern being IDC-NST is supported by both synthesis reports indicating high-grade features. However, Synthesis B's mention of subtle acinar variant architecture introduces ambiguity. The literature supports the primary diagnosis with similar high-grade invasive ductal carcinomas. Chain-of-thought reasoning for second diagnosis: Acinar cell carcinoma is considered due to the subtle acinar variant architecture mentioned by Synthesis B

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of infiltrating ductal carcinoma NST is supported by the presence of a high-grade invasive ductal carcinoma in the TCGA case TCGA-BR-A4QG, which exhibits similar morphological features such as SBR grade III and triple-negative-like behavior. The literature emphasizes the importance of grading systems like SBR for prognosis and treatment planning, highlighting the need for accurate assessment of nuclear grade and proliferation indices.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case provides a direct example of a high-grade invasive ductal carcinoma with similar characteristics to the working hypothesis.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of infiltrating ductal carcinoma NST, high grade, is supported by the presence of similar TCGA cases with high-grade IDC morphology. The use of automated proliferation indices may offer more precise grading than traditional mitotic counts. High-grade IDC is associated with triple-negative-like behavior and poor prognosis.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case matches the high-grade IDC morphology and provides survival data.

used_papers.title: Grading invasive ductal carcinoma of the breast: advantages of using automated proliferation index instead of mitotic count.
used_papers.snippet:

debate_history.diagnosis: Invasive ductal carcinoma, subtle acinar variant
debate_history.thinking: Chain-of-thought reasoning for primary diagnosis: The subtle acinar variant is supported by consistent glandular patterns with acinar features across multiple slides, despite the disagreement on cribriform areas. The borderline high-grade features suggest a more aggressive subtype within IDC. Against this, the lack of definitive cribriform areas and pancreatic-specific features makes other differentials less likely. For the second diagnosis, the cribriform variant is considered based on Histo-A'

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of glandular with subtle acinar variant is supported by the presence of a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma case (TCGA-PA-A5YG) with a multifocal pattern and grade II characteristics. Additionally, the invasive ductal carcinoma of breast (TCGA-BR-A4QG) shares some morphological features like high-grade characteristics and nuclear pleomorphism. These cases highlight the importance of immunohistochemistry for accurate differentiation.

used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
used_papers.relevance: This case provides a direct example of a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with multifocal and grade II characteristics.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case shares morphological features such as high-grade characteristics and nuclear pleomorphism, aiding in the understanding of the working hypothesis.

debate_rounds.argument: The primary diagnosis of Invasive ductal carcinoma of no special type (IDC-NST) is supported by the infiltrative growth pattern, high nuclear grade, and cribriform/glandular patterns. However, the discordant mitotic count in slide 7 and the ambiguous margin status required revisions. The differential diagnoses were considered based on the available evidence, with the second most likely being Invasive lobular carcinoma, pleomorphic variant, followed by Metaplastic carcinoma. The recommended IHC panel includes markers to confirm the diagnosis and assess hormone receptor status.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of infiltrating ductal carcinoma NST, cribriform, glandular is supported by the presence of similar cribriform growth patterns in TCGA-BR-DCIS-01 and the high-grade invasive ductal carcinoma in TCGA-BR-A4QG. The grading systems discussed in PMID 25763344 and PMID 17458558 highlight the importance of nuclear pleomorphism and mitotic counts in assessing tumor aggressiveness, which aligns with the high-grade characteristics seen in the patient.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: Similar cribriform growth patterns support the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: High-grade invasive ductal carcinoma with similar aggressive features.

debate_rounds.argument: Revised the primary diagnosis to reflect the potential for borderline high-grade features and the lack of consistent LVI confirmation. The grade was adjusted to SBR II to account for the increased nuclear pleomorphism and mitotic count, while acknowledging the subtle acinar variant morphology. The differential diagnosis includes invasive lobular carcinoma, pleomorphic variant, and metaplastic carcinoma, with supporting and against evidence provided for each. Recommended IHC markers include ER, PR, HER2, Ki-67, and E-cadherin to further confirm the diagnosis.

used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of infiltrating ductal carcinoma, no special type, is supported by the presence of similar TCGA cases with SBR grade III IDC. The literature emphasizes the importance of grading systems like SBR for prognosis and treatment planning, highlighting the need for accurate assessment of nuclear grade and proliferation.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case matches the high-grade IDC morphology and provides survival data.

debate_rounds.argument: The primary diagnosis of IDC-NST is supported by the multifocal distribution, lymphovascular invasion, and borderline high-grade features. However, the subtle acinar variant morphology and grade heterogeneity suggest a potential mixed-type carcinoma, leading to a revision of the grade to II. Further IHC markers such as CK7 and CK19 are recommended to clarify the differentiation.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case represents a high-grade IDC with similar morphological features and grading.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a solid growth pattern is supported by the presence of cribriform and solid growth patterns in the high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) case TCGA-BR-DCIS-01. The Ki-67 proliferation index is highlighted as a significant biomarker for grading and predicting outcomes across various tumor types, as seen in multiple studies. The solid growth pattern in DCIS suggests a more aggressive phenotype requiring careful monitoring and appropriate therapeutic interventions.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade DCIS, aligning with the working hypothesis.

suggested_papers.title: Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II

Female 64y. Invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type. 25mm. Single-file growth pattern, E-cadherin loss. Grade II. ER+, PR+, HER2-, Ki-67 12%. Multifocal pattern (3 foci). Margin involved laterally (R1). 0/10 nodes. pT2 pN0. Recommend repeat excision and adjuvant endocrine therapy
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a single-file growth pattern in lobular carcinoma, offering a contrast to the solid growth pattern in the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case matches the high-grade IDC morphology and provides survival data.

The differential diagnosis remains focused on invasive lobular carcinoma and metaplastic carcinoma, with supporting and against evidence provided for each. used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case exhibits similar morphological features to the working hypothesis, including solid and cribriform growth patterns.

suggested_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
suggested_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
suggested_papers.relevance: This case shows high-grade DCIS with cribriform and solid growth patterns, which may provide additional insights into the mixed patterns observed.

debate_history.thinking: Chain-of-thought reasoning for primary diagnosis: The dominant pattern described as solid growth with glandular and cribriform areas aligns well with IDC-NST. The high-grade features, lymphovascular invasion suspicion, and necrosis further support this diagnosis. However, the subtle acinar variant morphology mentioned in Synthesis B introduces ambiguity, suggesting the need for additional IHC markers to distinguish between IDC-NST and acinar cell carcinoma. The presence of borderline high-grade
debate_history.argument: Primary: Invasive ductal carcinoma of no special type (IDC-NST) — III (tubule formation: 3, nuclear pleomorphism: 3, mitoses: 3) — 8500/3

challenges: Grade III assignment may be inconsistent across slides
Ambiguous features regarding dominant pattern
debate_history.argument: Verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must justify grade III for the full case given discordant slide 7 mitotic count and clarify the dominant pattern discrepancy

debate_history.diagnosis: Invasive ductal carcinoma of no special type (IDC-NST)
debate_history.thinking: The revision addresses the QC feedback by adjusting the grade to Grade II based on the lower mitotic count in Slide 7 and acknowledging the dominant pattern discrepancy. The differential diagnosis now includes Acinar cell carcinoma and Pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma due to the subtle acinar variant morphology observed in all slides. The recommended IHC panel has been expanded to include CK7, CK19, and CA19-9 to further differentiate between the potential diagnoses.

challenges: Grade assignment inconsistency between DDx and Aggregator
Ambiguous features underweighted in DDx
debate_history.argument: Verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must justify grade assignment considering Aggregator's consolidated grade and address ambiguous features more thoroughly.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a solid growth pattern with glandular and cribriform areas is supported by the presence of similar morphologies in TCGA-BR-DCIS-01, which exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade DCIS. Additionally, TCGA-BR-A4QG shows an invasive ductal carcinoma with solid growth characteristics. These cases highlight the importance of assessing Ki-67 proliferation index for treatment planning.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: Exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade DCIS.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: Shows an invasive ductal carcinoma with solid growth characteristics.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of glandular with cribriform areas is supported by the presence of a high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) case with cribriform and solid growth patterns in the TCGA dataset. However, the differential diagnosis includes pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma, which shares some histological features but requires distinct immunohistochemical markers for accurate identification. The provided literature does not directly address the glandular with cribriform areas morphology but highlights the importance of distinguishing between various neoplasms.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This TCGA case provides a high-grade DCIS example with cribriform and solid growth patterns, supporting the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
used_papers.relevance: This TCGA case illustrates the differential diagnosis between pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma and acinar adenocarcinoma, emphasizing the need for immunohistochemical markers.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a solid growth pattern with subtle acinar variant is supported by the presence of multifocal grade II pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma in TCGA-PA-A5YG, which exhibits a similar solid growth pattern and perineural invasion. The high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (TCGA-BR-DCIS-01) also shows a cribriform and solid growth pattern, though it lacks an invasive component. The invasive ductal carcinoma of breast (TCGA-BR-A4QG) further supports the consideration of solid growth patterns in different contexts.

used_papers.relevance: This case demonstrates a solid growth pattern with perineural invasion in pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case shows a cribriform and solid growth pattern in high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case represents a high-grade IDC with similar morphological features and grading.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of spindle cell proliferation with myxoid change and solid growth pattern does not align well with the provided TCGA cases or PubMed articles. The closest differential diagnoses from the TCGA cases include IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis and pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma, both of which exhibit solid growth patterns but lack the specific spindle cell and myxoid features. The PubMed articles primarily focus on IgG4-related diseases and do not provide direct evidence for the current working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade DCIS, aligning with the working hypothesis.

suggested_papers.title: Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II

Female 64y. Invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type. 25mm. Single-file growth pattern, E-cadherin loss. Grade II. ER+, PR+, HER2-, Ki-67 12%. Multifocal pattern (3 foci). Margin involved laterally (R1). 0/10 nodes. pT2 pN0. Recommend repeat excision and adjuvant endocrine therapy
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a single-file growth pattern in lobular carcinoma, offering a contrast to the solid growth pattern in the working hypothesis.

ihc_recommended.rationale: To assess cell adhesion molecule expression

cap_report.comment: High-grade morphology with suspected lymphovascular invasion warrants further investigation. Molecular profiling recommended for KRAS/NRAS/BRAF/MSI given the high-grade features. Adjuvant therapy considerations based on final staging and biomarker results.

kras_nras_braf_panel.rationale: High-grade morphology suggests potential mutations

Subtle acinar variant architecture detected in all slides
Lymphovascular invasion suspected in some slides
High mitotic count up to 14/10HPF
against_evidence: No classic acinar pattern in all areas

differential_diagnosis.diagnosis: Invasive ductal carcinoma of no special type (IDC-NST)
supporting_evidence: Infiltrative growth pattern
High nuclear grade
Mitotic activity
High-grade morphology

BREAST TISSUE: INVASIVE DUCTAL CARCINOMA, ACINAR VARIANT, GRADE III (SBR), SIZE NOT SPECIFIED

Synoptic Report

Histologic TypeInvasive ductal carcinoma, acinar variant (8500/3)
GradeGrade III (SBR: T3+N3+M3=9/9)

Lymphovascular InvasionSuspected in 2/3 slides
Perineural InvasionNot identified

Necrosis PercentNot specified

  • E-cadherin — pending
  • CK7 — pending

QC Debate Summary

Verdict: revision_requested | Challenges:

  • [MAJOR] Grade III assignment may be inconsistent across slides
  • [MINOR] Discrepancies in margin status and lymphovascular invasion between histopathologists

Diagnostician proposed Invasive ductal carcinoma, acinar variant. QC verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must justify grade III for the full case given discordant slide 7 mitotic count; Cross-Slide-Aggregator should resolve margin and LVI discrepancies

Comment

High-grade morphology with suspected lymphovascular invasion warrants further investigation. Molecular profiling recommended for KRAS/NRAS/BRAF/MSI given the high-grade features. Adjuvant therapy considerations based on final staging and biomarker results.

AI-assisted analysis (PathMind v0.2 · AMD MI300X · 2026-05-09). Must be reviewed by a licensed pathologist before clinical use.

debate_rounds.argument: The primary diagnosis has been revised to 'Invasive ductal carcinoma, acinar variant' to better account for the subtle acinar variant architecture detected in all slides by one histopathologist. The grade has been reassessed to reflect the overall high-grade features while acknowledging the heterogeneity across slides. The differential diagnosis now gives more weight to the acinar variant, reducing the confidence in IDC-NST due to the presence of acinar features.

debate_history.thinking: Chain-of-thought reasoning for primary diagnosis: The dominant pattern identified as IDC-NST along with consolidated grade III and high mitotic count strongly support the primary diagnosis. However, the presence of acinar variant architecture in Histo-B introduces some ambiguity. The literature supports the working hypothesis of IDC-NST based on similar morphological features. literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of infiltrating ductal carcinoma NST is supported by the presence of a high-grade invasive ductal carcinoma in the TCGA case TCGA-BR-A4QG, which exhibits similar morphological features such as SBR grade III and triple-negative-like behavior. The literature emphasizes the importance of grading systems like SBR for prognosis and treatment planning, highlighting the need for accurate assessment of nuclear grade and proliferation indices.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case provides a direct example of a high-grade invasive ductal carcinoma with similar characteristics to the working hypothesis.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a solid growth pattern is supported by the presence of cribriform and solid growth patterns in the high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) case TCGA-BR-DCIS-01. The Ki-67 proliferation index is highlighted as a significant biomarker for grading and predicting outcomes across various tumor types, as seen in multiple studies. The solid growth pattern in DCIS suggests a more aggressive phenotype requiring careful monitoring and appropriate therapeutic interventions.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade DCIS, aligning with the working hypothesis.

suggested_papers.title: Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II

Female 64y. Invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type. 25mm. Single-file growth pattern, E-cadherin loss. Grade II. ER+, PR+, HER2-, Ki-67 12%. Multifocal pattern (3 foci). Margin involved laterally (R1). 0/10 nodes. pT2 pN0. Recommend repeat excision and adjuvant endocrine therapy
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a single-file growth pattern in lobular carcinoma, offering a contrast to the solid growth pattern in the working hypothesis.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of infiltrating ductal carcinoma NST, high grade, is supported by the presence of similar TCGA cases with high-grade IDC morphology. The use of automated proliferation indices may offer more precise grading than traditional mitotic counts. High-grade IDC is associated with triple-negative-like behavior and poor prognosis.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case matches the high-grade IDC morphology and provides survival data.

used_papers.title: Grading invasive ductal carcinoma of the breast: advantages of using automated proliferation index instead of mitotic count.

report.report_html:
BREAST EXCISION: INVASIVE DUCTAL CARCINOMA WITH SUBTLE ACINAR VARIANT ARCHITECTURE, GRADE II-III (SBR), MULTIFOCAL

Synoptic Report

Histologic TypeInvasive ductal carcinoma with subtle acinar variant architecture (8500/3)
GradeGrade II-III (SBR: T2+N0+M0=5/9)
Tumor Size MmNone
Lymphovascular InvasionSuspected
Perineural InvasionNot identified
Margins{"superior": "Not assessed", "inferior": "Not assessed", "medial": "Not assessed", "lateral": "Not assessed", "deep": "Not assessed", "superficial": "Not assessed"}
PtpT2
PnNot assessable from slides
Mitotic Count3-8 per 10HPF
Necrosis Percent5%
Tumor BuddingNot assessed
Stage GroupIIA
Msi PredictedNot assessed

IHC Panel (Recommended)

  • ER — pending
  • PR — pending
  • HER2 — pending
  • Ki-67 — pending
  • E-cadherin — pending
  • CK7 — pending
  • CK20 — pending
  • CK8/18 — pending
  • MUC1 — pending

QC Debate Summary

Verdict: revision_requested | Challenges:

  • [MAJOR] Grade assignment inconsistency between DDx and Cross-Slide-Aggregator
  • [MINOR] Ambiguous feature 'subtle acinar variant architecture' needs further confirmation

Diagnostician proposed Invasive ductal carcinoma with subtle acinar variant architecture. QC verdict: revision_requested. Differential-Diagnostician must reevaluate grade assignment and consider additional IHC markers for confirming subtle acinar variant architecture

Comment

Grade heterogeneity and subtle acinar variant architecture noted. CK8/18 and MUC1 IHC recommended for confirmation. KRAS/NRAS/BRAF/MSI panel suggested for molecular profiling. Adjuvant therapy should be considered based on final staging and biomarker results.

AI-assisted analysis (PathMind v0.2 · AMD MI300X · 2026-05-09). Must be reviewed by a licensed pathologist before clinical use.

debate_rounds.argument: Considering the grade heterogeneity and the consistent detection of subtle acinar variant architecture, the primary diagnosis has been revised to include this feature. The grade has been adjusted to reflect the borderline high-grade characteristics. The differential diagnosis now places greater emphasis on the subtle acinar variant architecture, acknowledging its potential impact on the final diagnosis.

[MINOR] Ambiguous feature 'subtle acinar variant architecture' needs further confirmation

debate_history.diagnosis: Invasive ductal carcinoma with acinar variant features
debate_history.thinking: Chain-of-thought reasoning for primary diagnosis: The dominant pattern described as 'glandular with subtle acinar variant architecture' along with the grade heterogeneity and multifocal distribution strongly supports a diagnosis of invasive ductal carcinoma with acinar variant features. The presence of lymphovascular invasion and necrosis further supports this diagnosis. However, the subtle acinar variant architecture raises the possibility of a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, although there i

Ambiguous features underweighted in DDx

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of glandular with subtle acinar variant architecture is supported by the presence of a TCGA case with pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma showing multifocal grade II features. Additionally, the TCGA case of invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III, shares some morphological characteristics such as high-grade features and nuclear pleomorphism. The literature does not directly address the specific glandular with subtle acinar variant architecture but provides insights into grading systems and differential diagnoses.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal, grade II

Patient: male, 65y. Pancreatic head mass 35mm. Whipple resection. Histology: infiltrating acinar adenocarcinoma, multifocal pattern, grade II (WHO 2022). Perineural invasion present, 3 foci. Lymphovascular invasion present. Margin: R1 anterior 0.4mm. pT2 pN1 (2/14
used_papers.relevance: This case illustrates a pancreatic acinar adenocarcinoma with multifocal grade II features, providing a potential differential diagnosis.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case highlights an invasive ductal carcinoma of breast with high-grade features, offering a comparison for morphological characteristics.

debate_rounds.argument: Considering the QC feedback, the grade was revised to II based on the overall mitotic activity and presence of necrosis in multiple slides. The subtle acinar variant architecture was given more weight in the differential diagnosis, leading to the inclusion of acinar cell carcinoma and pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma as less likely alternatives. The recommended IHC panel includes markers specific to these differentials to further clarify the diagnosis.

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of glandular with cribriform areas is supported by the presence of a high-grade ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) case with cribriform and solid growth patterns in the TCGA dataset. However, the differential diagnosis includes pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma, which shares some histological features but requires distinct immunohistochemical markers for accurate identification. The provided literature does not directly address glandular with cribriform areas but highlights the importance of distinguishing between various neoplasms.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case provides a high-grade DCIS example with cribriform and solid growth patterns, supporting the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
used_papers.relevance: This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma, emphasizing the need for immunohistochemical markers.

used_papers.title: Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade
used_papers.snippet:
Ductal carcinoma in situ, high grade

Female 52y. DCIS comedo type, high nuclear grade. 18mm. Cribriform and solid growth patterns. Central necrosis with calcifications. ER+, PR+. No invasive component identified after extensive sampling. Margin clear (4mm). Recommend lumpectomy + radiotherapy + endocrine therapy.
used_papers.relevance: This case exhibits cribriform and solid growth patterns in high-grade DCIS, aligning with the working hypothesis.

suggested_papers.title: Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II
suggested_papers.snippet:
Lobular carcinoma of breast, grade II

Female 64y. Invasive lobular carcinoma, classic type. 25mm. Single-file growth pattern, E-cadherin loss. Grade II. ER+, PR+, HER2-, Ki-67 12%. Multifocal pattern (3 foci). Margin involved laterally (R1). 0/10 nodes. pT2 pN0. Recommend repeat excision and adjuvant endocrine therapy
suggested_papers.relevance: This case involves a single-file growth pattern in lobular carcinoma, offering a contrast to the solid growth pattern in the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III
used_papers.snippet:
Invasive ductal carcinoma of breast, SBR grade III

Female 58y. IDC NOS, 22mm. SBR grade III: nuclear pleomorphism 3, tubular formation 3, mitotic count 14/10HPF. Lymphovascular invasion present. ER+, PR-, HER2-, Ki-67 35%. Triple-negative-like behavior. Margin clear (>5mm). 2/12 axillary nodes positive. pT2 pN1. 5y DF
used_papers.relevance: This case provides a direct example of a high-grade invasive ductal carcinoma with similar characteristics to the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Grading invasive ductal carcinoma of the breast: advantages of using automated proliferation index instead of mitotic count.
used_papers.snippet:
Grading invasive ductal carcinoma of the breast: advantages of using automated proliferation index instead of mitotic count.

Breast carcinomas are graded according to the "Nottingham modification of the Bloom-Richardson system" (SBR). The system is hindered, however, by lack of precision in assessing all three paramet
used_papers.journal: Virchows Archiv : an international journal of pathology
used_papers.relevance: This paper discusses the advantages of using automated proliferation index for grading invasive ductal carcinoma, which is relevant to the working hypothesis.

used_papers.title: Prognostic value of histologic grade nuclear components of Scarff-Bloom-Richardson (SBR). An improved score modification based on a multivariate analysis of 1262 invasive ductal breast carcinomas.
used_papers.snippet:
Prognostic value of histologic grade nuclear components of Scarff-Bloom-Richardson (SBR). An improved score modification based on a multivariate analysis of 1262 invasive ductal breast carcinomas.

We did a multivariate analysis of 1262 patients with operable, invasive ductal breast carcinoma to assess the prognostic v
used_papers.journal: Cancer
used_papers.relevance: This study evaluates the prognostic value of the SBR histologic grading system, which is crucial for understanding the prognosis of the working hypothesis.

suggested_papers.title: Scarff-Bloom-Richardson (SBR) grading: a pleiotropic marker of chemosensitivity in invasive ductal breast carcinomas treated by neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
suggested_papers.snippet:
Scarff-Bloom-Richardson (SBR) grading: a pleiotropic marker of chemosensitivity in invasive ductal breast carcinomas treated by neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

The Scarff-Bloom-Richardson (SBR) grade, an important prognostic factor in breast cancer, was also associated with cell proliferation, a consistent indicator of resp
suggested_papers.relevance: This paper explores the influence of SBR grade on response to neoadjuvant chemotherapy, which could be relevant for treatment considerations.

suggested_papers.title: A comparative and evaluative study of two cytological grading systems in breast carcinoma with histological grading: an important prognostic factor.
suggested_papers.snippet:
A comparative and evaluative study of two cytological grading systems in breast carcinoma with histological grading: an important prognostic factor.

Cytonuclear gradings in the breast carcinoma raise the level of FNA reportage and improves patient management. Our aim was to evaluate and compare two cytological grading
suggested_papers.relevance: This study compares two cytological grading methods with histological grading, providing additional insights into grading systems for breast carcinoma.

debate_history.diagnosis: Pancreatic Neuroendocrine Tumor (NET)
debate_history.thinking: Chain-of-thought reasoning for primary diagnosis: The glandular/subtle acinar pattern along with borderline high-grade features strongly suggest a pancreatic neuroendocrine tumor. The presence of necrosis and suggested perineural invasion further supports this diagnosis. However, the lack of specific mention of endocrine differentiation and absence of hormonal symptoms slightly weakens this conclusion. The differential diagnosis includes solid-pseudopapillary neoplasm and pancreatic adenocarcino

literature.key_findings: The working hypothesis of a glandular pattern suggests a differential diagnosis including pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (NETs) and solid-pseudopapillary neoplasms (SPNs). The TCGA case TCGA-PA-NEC-01 supports this hypothesis with its trabecular and solid growth pattern, while the literature highlights the importance of immunohistochemical markers like synaptophysin and chromogranin for distinguishing NETs from other glandular lesions.

used_papers.title: Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)
used_papers.snippet:
Pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma G2 (DDx case)

Male 59y. Pancreatic mass head, 30mm. Histology shows trabecular and solid growth pattern with monomorphic cells. Synaptophysin diffusely positive. Chromogranin positive. Ki-67 10% (G2 by WHO 2019 criteria). This case illustrates the differential diagnosis with acinar
used_papers.relevance: This TCGA case illustrates a pancreatic neuroendocrine carcinoma with a trabecular and solid growth pattern, supporting the glandular hypothesis.
